title: Massive UK Tax Rises!! FED Pause?, US-China Rare Earth Truce, Mag 7 Earnings, AI Bubble?
description:
Send a text
In this episode of Personable Markets, host Harvey Bracken-Smith and guest Luke Sloman analyze a defining week in global finance.
Nvidia hits a $5 trillion valuation, the Federal Reserve pauses rate cuts, and Trump and Xi Jinping outline a potential new trade framework that could reshape technology, agriculture, and rare-earth supply chains.

They explore the AI-driven surge in earnings from major tech firms, the growing dominance of the “BATMMAAN” stocks, and the UK’s controversial

title: How Remote Sales Pros Are Earning $30K p/m 2025 - Kris Cochrane & Sharni Campbell Reveal All
description:
There are people out there who’ve quit their job and are earning $30K+ a month with just a laptop and a set of specific skills.

The high-ticket sales industry is creating unprecedented income opportunities for people willing to master specialised remote selling skills.

Special guests and power couple Kris Cochrane and Sharni Campbell join us to reveal how their business helps people transition into this lucrative field - whether they're experienced salespeople or complete beginners looking to

title: The Long View: Interview with David Rubenstein, Cofounder, and Harvey Schwartz, CEO, of Carlyle
show: The Angle from T. Rowe Price
description: Eric Veiel, head of Global Investments and CIO at T. Rowe Price Associates, chats with David Rubenstein, Cofounder, and Harvey Schwartz, CEO, of Carlyle about its formation, the future for private investments, and their views on philanthropy.

title: Closing Bell: Playing Today’s Rally 3/23/26
description: Stocks jumped after President Trump said talks have happened with Iran. We break down all of those details and latest developments out of Washington. Fundstrat’s Tom Lee and CIBC Capital Markets’ Chris Harvey tell us how they are navigating these geopolitical uncertainties. Plus, Ashley MacNeill from Vista Equity Partners tells us if she thinks software has been oversold. And, our Closing Bell Headliner – Jeffery Gundlach from DoubleLine Capital.
